title: Ivory and gold flared lehenga for a nikah
concept_summary: A softly regal nikah concept built around a full-flared ivory lehenga with a fitted choli and a lightly draped dupatta over the head. Gold zardozi and dabka work catches the light while the overall mood stays restrained, luminous and ceremony-appropriate.

garment_breakdown.overall_form: A fitted choli paired with a voluminous circular lehenga skirt and a matching dupatta.
garment_breakdown.silhouette: A cleanly fitted upper body opening into a wide, evenly gathered floor-length flare.
garment_breakdown.drape_or_layering: The dupatta is draped over the head and falls along both sides of the body.
garment_breakdown.key_proportions: A close bodice, defined waist and a full skirt keep the proportions balanced and formal.

colour_story.palette_summary: An ivory base lifted by warm gold, kept soft rather than high-contrast.
colour_story.placement: Ivory leads across the skirt and choli, with gold as the accent through the embroidery and border.
colour_story.rationale: Ivory reads calm and bridal for a nikah, while gold adds warmth without overwhelming the look.

fabrics_and_texture.finish_and_movement: A smooth, gently structured drape that holds the skirt's flare.
A crisp, translucent layer that floats lightly over the head and shoulders.

embellishment_plan.density: A balanced amount of work concentrated at borders and the bodice rather than all over.
embellishment_plan.restraint_notes: Open ivory ground is left between motifs so the metalwork stays elegant, not heavy.

coverage_and_drape.neckline: A modest higher neckline that keeps the chest covered.
coverage_and_drape.back_and_midriff: A short choli leaves a natural midriff, styled to sit comfortably with the skirt.
coverage_and_drape.head_covering: The dupatta is worn over the head throughout, honouring the stated preference.
coverage_and_drape.dupatta_or_saree_drape: A single dupatta draped over the head and down both sides.

cultural_context.regional_direction: A broad Pakistani bridal influence guides the restraint and metalwork emphasis.
interpretation_notes: Treated as one broad regional direction, not a fixed rule for any community.
safeguards: No specific family, sect or regional tradition is presented as universal or definitive.

styling_notes: Keep jewellery warm-toned to echo the gold work.
A centre-parted, covered hairstyle suits the head drape.
construction_caveats: This is a concept visualisation, not a sewing pattern, and includes no measurements.
It does not guarantee that the garment can be constructed exactly as shown.
image_alt_text: A model in an ivory flared lehenga with gold embroidery and a sheer dupatta draped over the head for a nikah.
